sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: KB to cancel W2.3tr shares after record Q1 profit
feed.summary: KB Financial Group plans to cancel 14.26 million shares worth 2.3 trillion won ($1.55 billion) and approved an additional 600 billion won share buyback, demonstrating a commitment to shareholder returns. This announcement follows record first-quarter earnings, with net profit rising 11 percent to 1.89 trillion won, beating market expectations. The strong performance was driven by increased net fee income across its banking, securities, and asset management subsidiaries.

feed.title: DOL backs Morgan Stanley in deferred pay dispute, adding to pro-sponsor ERISA defenses
feed.summary: The Department of Labor (DOL) has sided with Morgan Stanley in a dispute over deferred pay, a move that is seen as reinforcing pro-sponsor protections under ERISA. This decision adds to a trend of DOL positions that observers believe benefit employers in certain retirement and compensation matters. The article, published on April 23, 2026, highlights this development in the context of broader trends in asset management and government policy.

feed.title: Bank of America (BAC) Remains in Warren Buffett’s Portfolio Since 2011; See Why
feed.summary: Warren Buffett has maintained Bank of America (BAC) in his portfolio since 2011, initially acquiring a $5 billion stake in preferred shares and later common shares in 2017. Despite some stake reduction, Buffett's investment in BAC totaled $28.45 billion as of Q4 2025, with the stock showing recent momentum. Analysts and management commentary highlight BAC's core strengths, including its strong deposit base, diversified revenue, consistent dividend growth, and share buyback capacity, reinforcing its long-term potential despite current market uncertainties.

feed.title: Principal Financial Group (NASDAQ:PFG) Sets New 1-Year High - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group (NASDAQ:PFG) shares reached a new 52-week high of $98.44 but last traded at $97.8950, prompting consideration for potential investors. The company recently declared a quarterly dividend of $0.80 per share, yielding 3.3% annually, and has a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average target price of $93.44. Insider selling has been noted, with CEO Deanna D. Strable-Soethout selling 9,300 shares, reducing her direct ownership.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Globe Life Q1 2026 misses EPS expectations, stock rises
feed.summary: Globe Life Inc. reported Q1 2026 earnings, missing EPS expectations slightly at $3.43 vs. $3.48 forecast, but revenue met projections at $1.56 billion. Despite the EPS miss, the stock rose 1.15% in premarket trading, driven by a 12% increase in net operating income per share year-over-year and investor optimism about strategic initiatives, including AI investments for cost reduction. The company maintains its dividend for 56 consecutive years and is pursuing share buybacks, while executives addressed questions on lapse rates, AI benefits, and Medicare Supplement sales dynamics.

feed.title: DTE links 2-year rate-request pause to data centers starting up
feed.summary: DTE Energy announced its intention to pause future electric rate requests for at least two years following an upcoming filing on April 28, 2026. This pause is contingent on the first supported data center coming online by the end of 2027 and other regulatory approvals. The company anticipates that two data center contracts will contribute nearly $9 billion to system improvements through 2045, helping to make energy more affordable for all customers and support grid investments.

feed.title: DTE Energy intends to pause future electric rate requests following upcoming filing as data centers come online
feed.summary: DTE Energy announced its intention to pause future electric rate requests for at least two years following a filing on April 28, 2026, with the Michigan Public Service Commission. This move is largely attributed to the affordability benefits generated by new data centers coming online, which will help spread fixed costs and reduce the financial burden on other customers. The company also highlighted ongoing investments in grid reliability and cleaner energy initiatives, including the conversion of the Belle River Power Plant and the development of the Trenton Channel Energy Center.

feed.title: Hilton, Olive deal sparks India hotel expansion with 10 new launches
feed.summary: Hilton Worldwide Holdings and Olive Hospitality have signed agreements for the first 10 Spark by Hilton hotels in India, advancing their 2024 plan to develop 150 franchised Spark hotels in the country. This expansion supports Hilton's growth in India's midscale segment, targeting both metro and non-metro locations such as Bengaluru, Jaipur, Nashik, Pune, Mathura, Rajkot, Hyderabad, and Goa. The Spark by Hilton brand focuses on simplicity and service, offering complimentary breakfast and a 24-hour retail market, contributing to Hilton's goal of exceeding 400 hotels in India in the coming years.

feed.title: Catalyst Pharmaceuticals stock hits all-time high at $26.63 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Catalyst Pharmaceuticals stock reached an all-time high of $26.63, trading within 1% of its 52-week high, driven by a 12.2% total return over the past year and a 25% gain in the last six months. The company's financial strength is highlighted by holding more cash than debt, and its stock appears undervalued based on InvestingPro analysis with a P/E ratio of 15.65 and a PEG ratio of 0.52. Recent strong Q4 2025 financial results, with EPS and revenue significantly exceeding forecasts, and ongoing research into Duchenne muscular dystrophy further contribute to investor confidence.

feed.title: Phillips 66 uses Jones Act waiver to ship Texas crude to East Coast refiner
feed.summary: Phillips 66 has utilized a Jones Act waiver to transport Texas crude oil to an East Coast refiner, marking the first use of the waiver since its issuance by President Trump in March 2026. This waiver, allowing foreign vessels to ship oil between U.S. ports, aims to alleviate supply disruptions caused by the conflict with Iran and is expected to exert downward pressure on WTI crude oil prices by easing supply constraints. Traders are now reassessing their positions as increased domestic shipping capacity could negate tension-driven price spikes.

feed.title: Clean Energy Fuels Names Clay Corbus CEO as Longtime Chief Andrew Littlefair Steps Aside
feed.summary: Clean Energy Fuels Corp. (NASDAQ: CLNE) has announced the appointment of Clay Corbus as its new President and CEO, effective immediately, succeeding co-founder Andrew Littlefair who led the company for three decades. Corbus, who has nearly two decades of experience with Clean Energy in senior leadership roles, will also join the Board of Directors. Littlefair will transition to a non-employee government relations consultant role while retaining his board seat, as the company continues to prioritize expansion of its renewable natural gas (RNG) platform in the transportation sector.

feed.title: Selling pressure pushes Global Payments stock lower in today's trading
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc (GPN) experienced a 5.30% drop in trading today, falling below its 20-day, 50-day, and 200-day Simple Moving Averages due to selling pressure. While some technical indicators suggest overbought conditions, others point to neutral momentum, creating a mixed signal for investors. Analysts from Traders Union foresee continued consolidation, with potential for further declines if the stock breaks below $67.33, or opportunities for recovery if it stabilizes above this level.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider City Holding (CHCO) After Recent Share Price Moves?
feed.summary: City Holding (CHCO) has seen mixed stock performance recently and is currently trading around US$124 per share. An "Excess Returns" analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 40.2%, estimating an intrinsic value of $208.02 per share. However, its P/E ratio of 13.82x is above industry and peer averages, and Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 9.51x indicates it may be overvalued by this metric.

feed.title: Has Clorox (CLX) Fallen Too Far After Its Prolonged Share Price Retreat?
feed.summary: Clorox (CLX) has experienced a significant share price retreat, prompting questions about its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is 33.6% undervalued at around $96.85, with an intrinsic value of $145.82 per share. The company's P/E ratio of 15.5x also sits below its industry and peer averages, indicating a potentially cautious market sentiment, and analyst narratives present both bullish and bearish cases for its future potential.

feed.title: West Pharmaceutical Stock Is Soaring After Earnings. It’s a Sneaky GLP-1 Play.
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services (WST) saw its stock soar after its recent earnings report, driven by its role in producing components for drug delivery, including GLP-1 weight-loss medications. While companies like Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk are well-known for GLP-1s, West Pharmaceutical presents a less obvious, yet significant, investment opportunity in the growing weight-loss drug market.

feed.title: Canadian National Railway Co. Is Maintained at Outperform by Raymond James
feed.summary: Raymond James has reiterated its "Outperform" rating for Canadian National Railway Co.

feed.title: Check Point stock hits 52-week low at 132.4 USD
feed.summary: Check Point Software Technologies Ltd. (CHKP) stock has dropped to a 52-week low of 132.36 USD, representing a -30.64% decline over the past year. Despite the downturn, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued with a P/E ratio of 13.97 and a PEG ratio of 0.49, and 18 analysts have revised earnings upwards. The company is actively pursuing strategic partnerships and launching new AI-focused advisory services to regain market momentum, even as Cantor Fitzgerald lowered its price target due to concerns about software multiple contraction.

feed.title: FDA approves Regeneron's gene therapy for inherited deafness
feed.summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved Regeneron's gene therapy, branded as Otarmeni, for a rare genetic form of deafness. The therapy will be offered for free to patients in the United States and marks the first gene therapy for genetic hearing loss to enter the market. Regeneron acquired ownership of the therapy when it purchased Decibel Therapeutics in 2023.

feed.title: ESS Maintained by Wells Fargo -- Price Target Lowered to $262
feed.summary: Wells Fargo maintained an Equal-Weight rating on Essex Property Trust (ESS) but lowered its price target to $262 from $268. According to GuruFocus, ESS is currently undervalued by 8.7% with a GF Value™ of $279.02 and boasts a strong GF Score™ of 80/100, indicating solid overall performance despite a weaker financial strength rating. No insider trading activity has been reported for ESS in the past three months.

feed.title: Hasbro delays financial results as cyberattack fallout drags into second quarter
feed.summary: Hasbro has delayed its financial results announcement due to the lingering effects of a recent cyberattack. The company released preliminary first-quarter revenue figures between $970 million and $985 million but has not yet estimated the full financial impact of the incident, nor held an analyst call. This ongoing issue highlights the difficulties Hasbro is facing, including previous legal and logistical challenges.

feed.title: Regeneron to offer newly approved deafness gene therapy free in U.S.
feed.summary: Regeneron has received accelerated FDA approval for Otarmeni (lunsotogene parvec-cwha), the first gene therapy specifically for OTOF-related severe-to-profound sensorineural hearing loss. The company plans to provide this treatment for free to eligible patients in the U.S. The approval is based on pivotal CHORD trial results, which demonstrated significant hearing improvements, including 80% of participants meeting the primary endpoint and 42% achieving normal hearing in longer follow-up.

feed.title: Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) Shares Gap Down - What's Next?
feed.summary: Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) shares gapped down significantly, opening at $385.00 after closing at $408.68, and are currently trading around $380.98 on heavy volume. Despite a mixed analyst outlook—UBS cut its price target, while the consensus remains a "Moderate Buy" with a $636.10 target—Intuit reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates and providing positive guidance for Q3 and FY2026. The company also recently declared a quarterly dividend of $1.20 per share.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin says Peru will buy 12 F-16 jets amid political dispute over deal (LMT:NYSE)
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin announced that Peru plans to purchase 12 F-16 Block 70 fighter jets to modernize its air force, despite political disputes surrounding the deal. The acquisition would benefit Lockheed Martin by securing a new strategic customer and expanding its presence in Latin America, while Peru aims to replace its aging fleet and enhance national airspace control. However, political uncertainty and a transitional government in Peru could pose challenges to the deal's funding and approval process.

feed.title: Huntington Reports Net Income of $523M in Q1
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares Inc. reported a Q1 2026 net income of $523 million, or 25 cents per common share, marking a 1% increase from the prior quarter. This performance includes $271 million in pretax notable items largely due to acquisition expenses from partnerships with Cadence Bank and Veritex Holdings Inc., with integration of Cadence expected to finalize in Q2 2026. The bank saw significant increases in net interest income, noninterest income, and average total loans, leases, and deposits, demonstrating strong organic growth and execution despite acquisition-related costs.

feed.title: Columbia Financial Names Splaine Principal Financial and Accounting Officer
feed.summary: Columbia Financial (CLBK) has appointed Executive Vice President and CFO Thomas Splaine, Jr. as its principal financial officer and principal accounting officer for SEC reporting, formalizing his role in financial reporting and compliance. This move follows his January 2026 CFO appointment and Dennis E. Gibney's promotion to Chief Banking Officer, enhancing the company's financial governance and banking strategy. An analyst maintains a Hold rating on CLBK with an $18.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark rates CLBK as Neutral due to cash-flow weakness, modest returns, and high P/E, although technicals and recent acquisitions provide some positive momentum.

feed.title: Nextera Energy stock reaches all-time high at 96.21 USD
feed.summary: Nextera Energy (NEE) stock has hit an all-time high of $96.21, reflecting strong investor confidence and the company's robust performance, including 30 consecutive years of dividend increases. Despite a recent revenue miss, the company surpassed first-quarter 2026 earnings expectations. This growth is attributed to strategic initiatives and a commitment to sustainable energy solutions, though InvestingPro suggests the stock may currently be overvalued.

feed.title: Lululemon shares drop after CEO change amplifies ongoing brand reset efforts
feed.summary: Lululemon's shares dropped nearly 12% after the company named former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its new CEO, signaling ongoing efforts to revive its U.S. business amidst weakening sales and increased competition. The leadership change comes as Lululemon's market value has significantly decreased, and analysts remain cautious about the effectiveness of these changes in addressing deeper operational concerns and a proxy fight led by founder Chip Wilson. The company is hoping O’Neill can strengthen product focus and re-energize its core customer base.

feed.title: C&F: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: C&F Financial Corp. (CFFI) reported a net income of $6.7 million in its first quarter, translating to earnings of $2.08 per share. The Toano, Virginia-based bank also posted revenue of $47.7 million, with revenue net of interest expense exceeding Street forecasts at $36.3 million. This financial update was generated by Automated Insights using Zacks Investment Research data.

feed.title: Pfizer Ends Early-Stage Cancer Trial, Trimming Its Oncology Pipeline
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. (PFE) has terminated its Phase 1 trial for the experimental cancer drug PF-08046037, which aimed to evaluate its efficacy as a monotherapy and in combination with sasanlimab for advanced malignancies. The decision is viewed as pipeline pruning rather than a major setback, as the program was early-stage and high-risk, suggesting Pfizer may reallocate resources to higher-conviction oncology programs. This move impacts company sentiment regarding R&D discipline and oncology focus but is unlikely to affect near-term earnings.

feed.title: Amgen Marks Key Milestone With Completion of Early MTAP-Null Cancer Combo Study
feed.summary: Amgen Inc. (AMGN) has completed a Phase 1/2 study for Anvumetostat in combination with IDE397, targeting MTAP-null solid tumors. The study focused on safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, and early efficacy. The completion of this early-stage trial signals data collection is finished, moving towards potential disclosure and further analysis for investors.

feed.title: Union Pacific Delivers Earnings Beat, Shares Move Higher On Strong Quarterly Results
feed.summary: Union Pacific reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, beating analyst expectations with diluted EPS of $2.87 and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.93. The company saw a 5% increase in net income and a 3% rise in operating revenue, while also improving operational efficiency and reaffirming its 2026 outlook for mid-single-digit EPS growth. Shares traded higher following the announcement, as the company also discussed its proposed merger with Norfolk Southern Corp and AI initiatives.

feed.title: City Opera House aims to implement sustainability with DTE Energy sponsorship
feed.summary: The City Opera House has partnered with DTE Energy as its first green sponsor to promote environmentally conscious operations. This collaboration will fund sustainability initiatives such as transitioning to rechargeable microphone systems and using recycled paper. Future plans include installing a bike rack to encourage alternative transportation, aligning the institution's artistic mission with environmental responsibility.

feed.title: Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) Announces Quarterly Earnings Results
feed.summary: Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) reported strong first-quarter earnings, beating analyst expectations with an adjusted EPS of $2.93 and revenue of $6.22 billion. The company highlighted record operational productivity and strong commercial momentum, reaffirming its mid-single-digit EPS growth target for 2026 and a multi-year high-single to low-double-digit EPS CAGR through 2027. Despite challenges like rising fuel costs and merger-related uncertainties, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock.

feed.title: ResMed Inc stock hits 52-week low at 218.27 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: ResMed Inc. stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $218.20, down 26% from its high, reflecting a 7.55% decline over the past year. Despite this downturn, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued, with management repurchasing shares. Recent Q2 fiscal 2026 earnings showed positive results, with EPS and revenue exceeding expectations, leading several analysts to raise their price targets for the company.

feed.title: CI&T To Announce First Quarter 2026 Results on May 11, 2026
feed.summary: CI&T Inc. (NYSE: CINT) announced that it will report its first quarter 2026 financial results after the market closes on May 11, 2026. The company's senior management team will host a video conference call on the same day at 4:30 PM Eastern Time / 5:30 PM BRT to discuss the results. The conference call will be accessible via their Investor Relations website or a provided YouTube link.

feed.title: Microsoft plans first voluntary employee buyout, CNBC reports
feed.summary: Microsoft is reportedly planning its first voluntary employee buyout in its 51-year history, targeting senior director-level employees and below in the U.S. whose age and years of employment total 70 or more. This comes as the tech giant has been aggressively investing in AI but is seeing slow adoption of its 365 Copilot service, and its stock has underperformed this year. The company is also making changes to its stock distribution for annual rewards, simplifying manager reviews, and consolidating its Copilot versions under its AI chief.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Stock Rallies After Q1 Beat On Strong Industrial And Data Center Demand
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (NASDAQ: TXN) stock rallied after reporting upbeat first-quarter results, driven by strong industrial and data center demand. Analysts from Rosenblatt Securities and JPMorgan reiterated their positive ratings and raised price targets for the company. The company reported impressive revenue growth and expanding gross margins, outperforming consensus estimates.

feed.title: Exxon, QatarEnergy ship first LNG cargo from Golden Pass export terminal (XOM:NYSE)
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil and QatarEnergy's joint venture, Golden Pass LNG, has shipped its first export cargo of liquefied natural gas from its Sabine Pass terminal in Texas. This marks a significant milestone for one of the largest new LNG export facilities.

feed.title: Assessing Canadian National Railway (TSX:CNR) Valuation After Recent Share Price Strength
feed.summary: Canadian National Railway (TSX:CNR) has experienced mixed short-term stock performance but shows positive longer-term returns. Its fair value is estimated at CA$132.87, suggesting it is currently overvalued compared to its market price of CA$149.68, despite having a strong, monopolistic business model. However, its P/E ratio is lower than the North American transportation average, prompting investors to consider if the market is being cautious or if it presents a buying opportunity.

feed.title: PNC to cut 777 FirstBank jobs this summer
feed.summary: PNC is set to eliminate 777 jobs at FirstBank's Lakewood, Colorado, headquarters following its recent acquisition. These reductions, primarily due to role overlap, will occur in two phases by late July, though some affected employees may find new roles within PNC. The bank emphasized its intention to retain all client-facing positions and establish Denver as a strategic technology hub, leveraging the local talent base.

feed.title: Conduent collaborates with EBT Community Council to advance program reliability
feed.summary: Conduent is participating in the EBTSummit2026 in Arlington, partnering with the EBT Community Council to improve the reliability and experience of Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) programs for states. The company will showcase its approaches to delivering better EBT solutions at its booth. This initiative aligns with Conduent's broader focus on organizational development and public sector programs, as evidenced by recent internal career growth stories.

feed.title: Columbia Financial names Thomas Splaine, Jr. as principal financial officer
feed.summary: Columbia Financial, Inc. (NASDAQ:CLBK) has designated Thomas Splaine, Jr., Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, as its principal financial officer and principal accounting officer for SEC reporting matters. This designation occurred on April 17, following his earlier appointment as EVP and CFO on January 28. The company is also set to acquire Northfield Bancorp Inc. in a merger valued at approximately $597 million, which will create the third largest regional bank headquartered in New Jersey.

feed.title: Petition asks Chemours to protect Okefenokee swamp
feed.summary: Conservationists delivered a petition with 26,000 signatures to Chemours, urging the company to permanently pledge against mining near the Okefenokee Swamp. The move comes after conservationists previously raised funds to buy land adjacent to the Okefenokee National Wildlife Refuge to prevent titanium dioxide harvesting. The petition highlights ongoing concerns about potential mining impacts on the sensitive ecosystem.

feed.title: Phillips 66 Sending US Oil on Foreign Vessel After Jones Waiver
feed.summary: Phillips 66 has begun shipping US oil from Texas to an East Coast refiner on a foreign-flagged vessel, following a waiver of the Jones Act by President Donald Trump. This marks the first such cargo of US crude on this route since the waiver. The oil, a Bakken light-sweet grade, was loaded onto the Malta-flagged Htm Warrior in early April and is destined for the Trainer refinery in Pennsylvania.

feed.title: Domino's cuts online pizza prices 50% for NFL Draft
feed.summary: Domino's is offering 50% off all menu-priced pizzas ordered online from April 20-26, coinciding with the NFL Draft. This deal includes various crust types and specialty pizzas. Additionally, customers ordering Handmade Pan or Parmesan Stuffed Crust online through June 14 will receive a free Domino's Slice Sauce.

feed.title: SPY vs. FIGB: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Provides Equity-Based Growth, While Fidelity Bond ETF Offers a Higher Yield
feed.summary: This article compares two ETFs, SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) and Fidelity Investment Grade Bond ETF (FIGB), highlighting their differing investment strategies and risk profiles. SPY offers equity-based growth with lower dividend yield and expense ratio, tracking the S&P 500, while FIGB provides higher income through investment-grade bonds but with a higher expense ratio. The analysis aims to guide investors on which ETF might be more suitable depending on their financial goals, whether growth or income.

feed.title: Lake County, Illinois, Sheriff’s Office Enhances Services With Tyler Technologies’ Public Safety Solutions
feed.summary: The Lake County Sheriff’s Office in Illinois has successfully implemented Tyler Technologies’ Enterprise Public Safety suite, including Enforcement Mobile, Civil Serve, and Corrections Mobile. This integration aims to enhance services for the community by improving accuracy, accessibility, and operational efficiency for officers. The new solutions reduce administrative burdens, minimize data entry, and provide real-time information, allowing officers to focus on critical safety issues.

feed.title: Domino's cuts online pizza prices 50% for NFL Draft
feed.summary: Domino's is offering a 50% discount on all menu-priced pizzas ordered online in anticipation of the NFL Draft, which runs from April 20-26. This promotion includes various crust types and is aimed at enhancing draft-day gatherings for fans. Additionally, customers ordering Handmade Pan or Parmesan Stuffed Crust pizzas online can receive a free Domino's Slice Sauce through June 14.

feed.title: Union Pacific Corp Stock (UNP) Moved Up by 7.06% on Apr 23: Key Drivers Unveiled
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corp (UNP) stock increased by 7.06% on April 23, driven by a strong first-quarter 2026 earnings report that surpassed analyst expectations and highlighted significant operational efficiencies. The company reported growth in operating and freight revenue, despite a slight decrease in carloads, attributed to core pricing gains and fuel surcharges. UNP also reaffirmed a positive 2026 outlook, projecting mid-single-digit EPS growth and continued operating ratio improvements, further boosting investor confidence.

feed.title: Cass Information Systems (NASDAQ:CASS) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.04 EPS
feed.summary: Cass Information Systems (NASDAQ:CASS) reported quarterly earnings of $0.66 EPS, surpassing analyst expectations by $0.04, though revenue of $26.16 million significantly missed the $49.40 million consensus. Despite the EPS beat, shares fell by $1.64 to $46.83, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $50.00. The company, which provides payment and information processing services, has a market cap of $604.46 million and a P/E of 17.89.
